message.content:
Here are some good, budget‑friendly tool brands that are reliable enough for beginners without getting into “cheap junk” territory:

• Ryobi – Probably the best value-for-money starter brand. Huge range of tools, decent quality, very beginner‑friendly, and compatible batteries.  
• Craftsman – Solid middle‑ground brand with reasonable prices. Good for basic home and garage projects.  
• Stanley – Good for hand tools (hammers, screwdrivers, tape measures, etc.). Affordable and dependable.  
• Kobalt – Lowe’s in‑house brand. Nice price-to-performance ratio, especially for power tools and mechanics’ tools.  
• Harbor Freight’s Hercules or Bauer lines – Much better than their lowest-tier stuff. Good option when you need cheap but capable tools.  
• Black+Decker – Entry-level power tools. Not for heavy use, but perfectly fine for light DIY.  
• Hart – Walmart’s tool line. Surprisingly decent for budget users.

If you want the “best” long‑term value without going into expensive pro brands, Ryobi is often considered the sweet spot.

If you want, tell me what kind of projects you’re planning (woodworking, basic home repairs, automotive, etc.), and I can tailor the recommendations.
